seoTitle: How to Turn Off a Payment Term in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Disable an unused payment term in QuickBooks Online to simplify invoicing and reduce confusion for clients on billing schedules.
intro: Simplifying your invoicing by turning off payment terms you no longer use can reduce confusion for clients and streamline your billing process. This guide walks through disabling a payment term in QuickBooks Online and understanding its effect on invoicing.

steps.heading: Navigate to payment term settings
steps.body: Open the settings area in QuickBooks Online where payment terms are listed and managed.

steps.heading: Select the payment term to disable
steps.body: Find the specific payment term you want to turn off from the list of available options.

steps.heading: Turn off the payment term
steps.body: Use the setting or toggle to disable the term so it's no longer selectable on new invoices.

steps.heading: Review existing invoices using that term
steps.body: Check whether any active invoices currently use the disabled term, since turning it off typically doesn't affect existing invoices.

steps.heading: Confirm the change
steps.body: Save your settings so the payment term no longer appears as an option going forward.

faq.question: Does turning off a payment term affect invoices that already use it?
faq.answer: No, existing invoices generally keep the payment term they were created with; the change only affects new invoices going forward.

faq.question: Why would a business want to turn off a payment term?
faq.answer: Businesses often simplify their billing by removing payment terms they no longer offer, reducing the chance of client confusion.

faq.question: Can I turn a disabled payment term back on later?
faq.answer: Yes, disabled payment terms can typically be reactivated if you decide to offer that term again in the future.

faq.question: How does managing payment terms affect cash flow?
faq.answer: Simplifying and standardizing payment terms can make it easier to predict when payments will arrive, supporting better cash flow management.
